Things the garden taught me

grow slowly, and bloom anyway.

Bloom where you're planted

you don't need perfect soil to start — you just need to begin.

Tend the small things

one green shoot. one ripe tomato. that quiet little win is the whole point.

Some things grow in their own time

you can't rush a root — patience is its own harvest.

cutting something back isn't loss — it's how you make room for what's coming next.
